content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the right ahead with the white boundary line visible on the left edge — the car appears to be on the outer (right) side of a left-to-right curve, heading toward the inside of the turn.

**Waypoint analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 83, target index = (83+5) % 121 = 88
- WP88: x=0.8921, y=4.1362
- Car: x=1.0557, y=4.3792, heading=-179.853°
- dy = 4.1362 - 4.3792 = -0.2430
- dx = 0.8921 - 1.0557 = -0.1636
- bearing = atan2(-0.2430, -0.1636) → third quadrant: base angle = atan(0.2430/0.1636) = 56.1°, bearing = -(180° - 56.1°) = **-123.9°**
- delta = -123.9° - (-179.853°) = +55.95° → LEFT correction needed

**Boundary check:**
- track_width/2 = 0.3799m, distance_from_center = 0.0623m, is_left_of_center = false (right of center)
- edge_distance (right side) = 0.3799 - 0.0623 = **0.3176m** → well clear
- delta = +55.95° → steer left, away from right edge → no boundary concern

Delta ≈ +56°: |delta| > 50°, steer left: steering_angle=+25, speed=0.75.
